chain_edges.notes: Hyperscaler-lab exclusives with co-designed silicon (Trainium/TPU/MAIA) feed the architectural premise that custom chips diverge into training vs inference SKUs.
Hyperscaler-lab codesign regime extends naturally to robot training consolidating on the same hyperscaler infrastructure.
Training-vs-inference SKU split underpins the 'robot as moving compute endpoint' framing: training centralizes, inference decentralizes.
Agent-in-the-loop secret exfiltration via CI generalizes to OAuth-mediated AI-SaaS exfiltration; same threat class, broader surface.
Indirect prompt injection trend feeds OAuth-trust risk: AI integrations become privileged execution paths admins must scope down.
Agent Registry's tool-permissions/audit-trace standardization motivates SSO carving an AI-extension OAuth scope.

relations.notes: Both describe hyperscaler in-house silicon strategy; this one focuses on training/inference SKU split, the other on lab-cloud-chip pole exclusives.
Both reflect cloud-side training consolidation with edge-side inference; robotics is a domain instance of the silicon SKU split.
Hyperscaler consolidation theme: lab-cloud-chip exclusives parallel robot-training-cloud consolidation.
Different workload class choices: coding agents go local-first; robot training stays cloud-consolidated.
Robot-as-SaaS extends the AI-SaaS enterprise contract surface that OAuth-trust risk also targets.
Both name a new agent/AI-integration security category; CI-secret leak vs OAuth-scoped SaaS pivot are independent facets.
Indirect prompt injection CVEs and OAuth-trust controls are adjacent enterprise AI-security strands.
Agent Registry permissions/audit and SSO AI-extension OAuth scope are complementary control planes for agent governance.
